acceptance_criteria.criterion: Prioritized list of what to build or fix next
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Two categories: Architectural (A1-A5) and Enforcement (E1-E3), each with problem statement, deliverables, and effort estimates. 3-phase build order with dependencies

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Identifies what to explicitly defer or drop
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Defer table (9 items with rationale) and Drop/Simplify table (4 items). E.g., 'Agent SDK adapter completion — Defer unless actively using Agent SDK'

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Notes what is working well and should be preserved
acceptance_criteria.evidence: 8 enumerated items with justifications. E.g., 'Context budget enforcement — 300-line ceiling with measurement. The #1 v1 lesson. Protect it.'

dimensions.name: coverage
dimensions.evidence: Recommendations cover all major gap categories from gap-matrix. Defer list addresses Phase 4-6 plan items

dimensions.name: depth
dimensions.evidence: Each recommendation specifies concrete deliverables with estimated scope. A1: '~80 lines of shell added to step-transition.sh or a new validate-step-artifacts.sh script'

dimensions.name: sources
dimensions.evidence: Recommendations trace to gap-matrix findings. A2 references adapters/shared/schemas/plan.schema.json. E1 references 'Two absent gap-review behaviors (#5, #13)'

dimensions.name: synthesis
dimensions.evidence: Build order synthesizes dependencies: 'Phase I: A1, A2, A3 in parallel. Phase II: A4 depends on A2, A5 depends on A4. Phase III: E2 depends on A1'

dimensions.name: actionability
dimensions.evidence: Highest actionability of the three. Each item includes problem, files to create/modify, line estimates, and scope control ('What NOT to build')
